title: Ne-Yo

infobox.value: Shaffer Chimere Smith   (1979-10-18) October 18, 1979 (age 46)[[1]](#cite_note-birth-1)  [Camden, Arkansas](/wiki/Camden,_Arkansas "Camden, Arkansas"), U.S.
* Singer * songwriter * record producer * dancer * actor
[Full list](/wiki/List_of_awards_and_nominations_received_by_Ne-Yo "List of awards and nominations received by Ne-Yo")

(born October 18, 1979), known professionally as **Ne-Yo** ([/ˈniːjoʊ/](/wiki/Help:IPA/English "Help:IPA/English") [*NEE-yoh*](/wiki/Help:Pronunciation_respelling_key "Help:Pronunciation respelling key")), is an American singer, songwriter, and record producer. Regarded as a preeminent figure in [2000s R&B music](/wiki/Contemporary_R%26B#2000s "Contemporary R&B"),[[7]](#cite_note-7)[[8]](#cite_note-8)[[9]](#cite_note-9)[[10]](#cite_note-10) he is the recipient of numerous accolades, including three [Grammy Awards](/wiki/Grammy_Awards "Grammy Awards"). He gained recognition for his songwriting abilities following the success of his first major credit, [Mario](/wiki/Mario_(American_singer) "Mario (American singer)")'s 2004 single "[Let Me Love You](/wiki/Let_Me_Love_You_(Mario_song) "Let Me Love You (Mario song)")". Its release prompted a meeting between Ne-Yo and [Def Jam](/wiki/Def_Jam_Recordings "Def Jam Recordings")'s then-president [Jay-Z](/wiki/Jay-Z "Jay-Z"), resulting in a contract in which he released four studio albums, each of which spawned [hit songs](/wiki/Hit_song "Hit song").

and joined an R&B group called Envy, who appeared during amateur night on *[Showtime at the Apollo](/wiki/Showtime_at_the_Apollo "Showtime at the Apollo")*[[18]](#cite_note-18) and on the short lived [MTV](/wiki/MTV "MTV") series *[The Cut](/wiki/The_Cut_(1998_TV_series) "The Cut (1998 TV series)")*[[19]](#cite_note-19) (hosted by [Lisa "Left Eye" Lopes](/wiki/Lisa_Lopes "Lisa Lopes") of [TLC](/wiki/TLC_(group) "TLC (group)")).[[20]](#cite_note-20) The group disbanded in 2000, and Smith continued to write songs for other artists before starting his solo career. The stage name **Ne-Yo** was coined by Big D Evans, a producer with whom Ne-Yo once worked,[[21]](#cite_note-Vibe20Q-21) because Evans claimed that Ne-Yo sees music like the character [Neo](/wiki/Neo_(The_Matrix) "Neo (The Matrix)") sees [the Matrix](/wiki/Matrix_(fictional_universe) "Matrix (fictional universe)").[[22]](#cite_note-22)

**Shaffer Chimere Smith** (born October 18, 1979), known professionally as **Ne-Yo** ([/ˈniːjoʊ/](/wiki/Help:IPA/English "Help:IPA/English") [*NEE-yoh*](/wiki/Help:Pronunciation_respelling_key "Help:Pronunciation respelling key")), is an American singer, songwriter, and record producer. Regarded as a preeminent figure in [2000s R&B music](/wiki/Contemporary_R%26B#2000s "Contemporary R&B"),[[7]](#cite_note-7)[[8]](#cite_note-8)[[9]](#cite_note-9)[[10]](#cite_note-10) he is the recipient of numerous accolades, including three [Grammy Awards](/wiki/Grammy_Awards "Grammy Awards"). He gained recognition for his songwriting abilities following the success of his first major credit, [Mario](/wiki/Mario_(American_singer) "Mario (American singer)")'s 2004 single "[Let Me Love You](/wiki/Let_Me_Love_You_(Mario_song) "Let Me Love You (Mario song)")". Its release prompted a meeting between Ne-Yo and [Def Jam](/wiki/Def_Jam_Recordings "Def Jam Recordings")'s then-president [Jay-Z](/wiki/Jay-Z "Jay-Z"), resulting in a contract in which he released four studio albums, each of which spawned [hit songs](/wiki/Hit_song "Hit song").

Ne-Yo's 2005 debut single, "[Stay](/wiki/Stay_(Ne-Yo_song) "Stay (Ne-Yo song)")" (featuring [Peedi Peedi](/wiki/Peedi_Peedi "Peedi Peedi")), was released the following year to lukewarm commercial reception, though his 2005 single, "[So Sick](/wiki/So_Sick "So Sick")", became his [breakthrough](/wiki/Breakthrough_role "Breakthrough role") hit as it peaked atop the [*Billboard* Hot 100](/wiki/Billboard_Hot_100 "Billboard Hot 100"). Both songs, along with the follow-up singles "[When You're Mad](/wiki/When_You%27re_Mad "When You're Mad")" and "[Sexy Love](/wiki/Sexy_Love "Sexy Love")", preceded the release of his debut studio album *[In My Own Words](/wiki/In_My_Own_Words "In My Own Words")* (2006), which peaked atop the [*Billboard* 200](/wiki/Billboard_200 "Billboard 200") and saw positive critical reception. Furthermore, the album yielded two nominations at the [49th Annual Grammy Awards](/wiki/49th_Annual_Grammy_Awards "49th Annual Grammy Awards") and received [platinum](/wiki/RIAA_certification "RIAA certification") certification by the [Recording Industry Association of America](/wiki/Recording_Industry_Association_of_America "Recording Industry Association of America") (RIAA), in addition to similar certifications in the UK, Japan and Australia.

His second and third albums, *[Because of You](/wiki/Because_of_You_(Ne-Yo_album) "Because of You (Ne-Yo album)")* (2007) and *[Year of the Gentleman](/wiki/Year_of_the_Gentleman "Year of the Gentleman")* (2008), were met with continued success, peaking at numbers one and two on the *Billboard* 200, respectively. The former won [Best Contemporary R&B Album](/wiki/Grammy_Award_for_Best_Contemporary_R%26B_Album "Grammy Award for Best Contemporary R&B Album") at the [50th Annual Grammy Awards](/wiki/50th_Annual_Grammy_Awards "50th Annual Grammy Awards"), while the latter was nominated for the award, as well as for [Album of the Year](/wiki/Grammy_Award_for_Album_of_the_Year "Grammy Award for Album of the Year"). They spawned the US-top ten singles "[Because of You](/wiki/Because_of_You_(Ne-Yo_song) "Because of You (Ne-Yo song)")", "[Closer](/wiki/Closer_(Ne-Yo_song) "Closer (Ne-Yo song)")", and "[Miss Independent](/wiki/Miss_Independent_(Ne-Yo_song) "Miss Independent (Ne-Yo song)")" — the latter won [Best Male R&B Vocal Performance](/wiki/Best_Male_R%26B_Vocal_Performance "Best Male R&B Vocal Performance") and [Best R&B Song](/wiki/Grammy_Award_for_Best_R%26B_Song "Grammy Award for Best R&B Song") at the [51st Annual Grammy Awards](/wiki/51st_Annual_Grammy_Awards "51st Annual Grammy Awards"). *Year of the Gentleman* received double platinum certification by the RIAA—selling two million copies—as well as triple platinum certification by the [British Phonographic Industry](/wiki/British_Phonographic_Industry "British Phonographic Industry") (BPI).

*[Libra Scale](/wiki/Libra_Scale "Libra Scale")* (2010), Ne-Yo's fourth studio album, was met with moderate success on international charts and reached number nine on the US *Billboard* 200. Seeing an overall decline in commercial reception, he parted ways with Def Jam in favor of its coequal [Universal Music Group](/wiki/Universal_Music_Group "Universal Music Group") subsidiary, [Motown](/wiki/Motown "Motown") two years later. His fifth album, *[R.E.D.](/wiki/R.E.D._(Ne-Yo_album) "R.E.D. (Ne-Yo album)")* (2012) peaked at number four on the chart and was preceded by the single "[Let Me Love You (Until You Learn to Love Yourself)](/wiki/Let_Me_Love_You_(Until_You_Learn_to_Love_Yourself) "Let Me Love You (Until You Learn to Love Yourself)")", which peaked at number six on the *Billboard* Hot 100 and peaked atop the [UK Singles Chart](/wiki/UK_Singles_Chart "UK Singles Chart"). His sixth album, *[Non-Fiction](/wiki/Non-Fiction_(Ne-Yo_album) "Non-Fiction (Ne-Yo album)")* (2015) was met with similar commercial response despite a steep critical decline. It was supported by the *Billboard* Hot 100-top ten single "[Time of Our Lives](/wiki/Time_of_Our_Lives_(Pitbull_and_Ne-Yo_song) "Time of Our Lives (Pitbull and Ne-Yo song)")" (with [Pitbull](/wiki/Pitbull_(rapper) "Pitbull (rapper)")) and the [strip club](/wiki/Strip_club "Strip club")-inspired top 40 single, "[She Knows](/wiki/She_Knows_(Ne-Yo_song) "She Knows (Ne-Yo song)")" (featuring [Juicy J](/wiki/Juicy_J "Juicy J")). His seventh album, *[Good Man](/wiki/Good_Man_(album) "Good Man (album)")* (2018), peaked at number 33 on the *Billboard* 200, while his eighth and ninth albums, *Another Kind of Christmas* (2019) and *[Self Explanatory](/wiki/Self_Explanatory_(Ne-Yo_album) "Self Explanatory (Ne-Yo album)")* (2022), narrowly entered the chart.[[11]](#cite_note-11)[[12]](#cite_note-12)

Alongside his recording career, Ne-Yo served as a judge for the [reality competition](/wiki/Reality_competition "Reality competition") series *[World of Dance](/wiki/World_of_Dance_(TV_series) "World of Dance (TV series)")* from 2017 to 2020 alongside [Jennifer Lopez](/wiki/Jennifer_Lopez "Jennifer Lopez") and [Derek Hough](/wiki/Derek_Hough "Derek Hough"). In 2021, Ne-Yo competed as Badger on the second season of *[The Masked Singer UK](/wiki/The_Masked_Singer_(British_TV_series) "The Masked Singer (British TV series)"),* and finished in second place. In 2023, he competed on the tenth season of *[The Masked Singer](/wiki/The_Masked_Singer_(American_TV_series) "The Masked Singer (American TV series)"),* as the Cow and finished in first place. According to [Recording Industry Association of America](/wiki/Recording_Industry_Association_of_America "Recording Industry Association of America"), Ne-Yo has sold 29 million certified albums and singles in the United States.[[13]](#cite_note-13) *[Billboard](/wiki/Billboard_(magazine) "Billboard (magazine)")* ranked "[Give Me Everything](/wiki/Give_Me_Everything "Give Me Everything")" and "[Knock You Down](/wiki/Knock_You_Down "Knock You Down")" among the Greatest Songs of the Summer of all time.[[14]](#cite_note-14) Ne-Yo has yielded five number one singles on the UK Singles Chart, and spent 315 weeks within the chart's Top 75.[[15]](#cite_note-15) Ne-Yo also has four songs listed on Motown's Top 100 songs of the Millennium in the UK—the fifth most entries on the list.[[16]](#cite_note-16)

## Early life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ne-Yo&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early life")]

Shaffer Chimere Smith was born on October 18, 1979, in [Camden, Arkansas](/wiki/Camden,_Arkansas "Camden, Arkansas") to parents Lorraine and Shaffer Smith, both of whom are also musicians. As a young child, he was raised by his mother after she separated from his father.[[17]](#cite_note-LoNe-Yo-17) His mother then relocated the family to [Las Vegas](/wiki/Las_Vegas "Las Vegas"), Nevada.

While in the [Las Vegas Academy](/wiki/Las_Vegas_Academy "Las Vegas Academy"), Smith adopted the stage name **GoGo** and joined an R&B group called Envy, who appeared during amateur night on *[Showtime at the Apollo](/wiki/Showtime_at_the_Apollo "Showtime at the Apollo")*[[18]](#cite_note-18) and on the short lived [MTV](/wiki/MTV "MTV") series *[The Cut](/wiki/The_Cut_(1998_TV_series) "The Cut (1998 TV series)")*[[19]](#cite_note-19) (hosted by [Lisa "Left Eye" Lopes](/wiki/Lisa_Lopes "Lisa Lopes") of [TLC](/wiki/TLC_(group) "TLC (group)")).[[20]](#cite_note-20) The group disbanded in 2000, and Smith continued to write songs for other artists before starting his solo career. The stage name **Ne-Yo** was coined by Big D Evans, a producer with whom Ne-Yo once worked,[[21]](#cite_note-Vibe20Q-21) because Evans claimed that Ne-Yo sees music like the character [Neo](/wiki/Neo_(The_Matrix) "Neo (The Matrix)") sees [the Matrix](/wiki/Matrix_(fictional_universe) "Matrix (fictional universe)").[[22]](#cite_note-22)

## Music career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ne-Yo&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Music career")]

### 1998–2005: Career beginnings

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ne-Yo&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: 1998–2005: Career beginnings")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/1b/NeYoJul05.jpg/250px-NeYoJul05.jpg)](/wiki/File:NeYoJul05.jpg)

Ne-Yo performing in July 2005

After Envy disbanded in 2000, [Columbia Records](/wiki/Columbia_Records "Columbia Records") signed Ne-Yo, but the label dropped him before he could release his already-recorded first album.[[23]](#cite_note-JonesTunes-23) American singer [Marques Houston](/wiki/Marques_Houston "Marques Houston") happened to hear "[That Girl](/wiki/That_Girl_(Marques_Houston_song) "That Girl (Marques Houston song)")", which Ne-Yo had planned to release as his debut [single](/wiki/Single_(music) "Single (music)") from his then-unreleased album. Houston re-recorded the song and released it as a single for his 2003 album *[MH](/wiki/MH_(Marques_Houston_album) "MH (Marques Houston album)")*.[[24]](#cite_note-ReevesSlick-24) The release of the song led to Ne-Yo being recognized as a top songwriter.[[24]](#cite_note-ReevesSlick-24)

For the next two years, Ne-Yo continued writing songs, some of which have not been officially released.[[24]](#cite_note-ReevesSlick-24) He contributed songs to American singer [Teedra Moses](/wiki/Teedra_Moses "Teedra Moses")'s 2004 album *[Complex Simplicity](/wiki/Complex_Simplicity "Complex Simplicity")*, [Christina Milian](/wiki/Christina_Milian "Christina Milian")'s *[It's About Time](/wiki/It%27s_About_Time_(Christina_Milian_album) "It's About Time (Christina Milian album)")*, and the American boy band [Youngstown](/wiki/Youngstown_(band) "Youngstown (band)"),[[25]](#cite_note-AMGBio-25) none of which, however, brought Ne-Yo much mainstream attention.[[24]](#cite_note-ReevesSlick-24) Ne-Yo has also contributed songs to singers [Mary J. Blige](/wiki/Mary_J._Blige "Mary J. Blige"), [B2K](/wiki/B2K "B2K"), [Faith Evans](/wiki/Faith_Evans "Faith Evans"), and [Musiq](/wiki/Musiq_Soulchild "Musiq Soulchild"), among others.[[26]](#cite_note-ShaheemPicks-26)

A breakthrough came when Ne-Yo wrote "[Let Me Love You](/wiki/Let_Me_Love_You_(Mario_song) "Let Me Love You (Mario song)")" for American singer [Mario](/wiki/Mario_(American_singer) "Mario (American singer)"). The song reached number one on the [*Billboard* Hot 100](/wiki/Billboard_Hot_100 "Billboard Hot 100"), and later stayed at the top spot for nine weeks.[[25]](#cite_note-AMGBio-25) After the successful release, Tina Davis, former A&R representative for [Def Jam Recordings](/wiki/Def_Jam_Recordings "Def Jam Recordings"), arranged an informal meeting with label head [L.A. Reid](/wiki/L.A._Reid "L.A. Reid").[[24]](#cite_note-ReevesSlick-24) Despite not seeking a new contract at the time, then-CEO of Def Jam [Jay-Z](/wiki/Jay-Z "Jay-Z") signed Ne-Yo to a new deal after he performed for the label's executives.[[23]](#cite_note-JonesTunes-23)[[26]](#cite_note-ShaheemPicks-26)

### 2006–2007: *In My Own Words* and *Because of You*

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ne-Yo&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: 2006–2007: In My Own Words and Because of You")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/40/Ne-Yo_Promotion.jpg/250px-Ne-Yo_Promotion.jpg)](/wiki/File:Ne-Yo_Promotion.jpg)

Ne-Yo promoting *[Because of You](/wiki/Because_of_You_(Ne-Yo_album) "Because of You (Ne-Yo album)")* in 2007

In early 2006, Ne-Yo released his debut album, *[In My Own Words](/wiki/In_My_Own_Words "In My Own Words")*, through [Def Jam](/wiki/Def_Jam "Def Jam"). Boosted by its popular second single "[So Sick](/wiki/So_Sick "So Sick")", the album debuted at number one on [*Billboard* 200](/wiki/Billboard_200 "Billboard 200"), selling over 301,000 copies in its first week on sale.[[27]](#cite_note-27) During the same week, the single had reached number one on the *Billboard* Hot 100.[[28]](#cite_note-HopeTakeover-28) Later singles released were "[When You're Mad](/wiki/When_You%27re_Mad "When You're Mad")" and "[Sexy Love](/wiki/Sexy_Love "Sexy Love")", which peaked at number 15 and number seven, respectively.[[29]](#cite_note-BillChartHis-29) The album has been certified platinum by the [Recording Industry Association of America](/wiki/Recording_Industry_Association_of_America "Recording Industry Association of America") (RIAA) for a shipment of over one million units.[[30]](#cite_note-RIAA-30)

His second album, *[Because of You](/wiki/Because_of_You_(Ne-Yo_album) "Because of You (Ne-Yo album)")*, was released on May 1, 2007, and, fueled by its lead single, debuted at number one on the *Billboard* 200, selling 251,000 copies in the United States; the feat gave Ne-Yo his second number-one album.[[31]](#cite_note-31) The first single from the platinum-selling album was the [title track](/wiki/Because_of_You_(Ne-Yo_song) "Because of You (Ne-Yo song)"), which peaked at the number-two spot.[[29]](#cite_note-BillChartHis-29) Despite the success of "Because of You", later singles released charted lower and were unable to reach the Top 20.[[29]](#cite_note-BillChartHis-29) The album has been certified platinum by RIAA for a shipment of over one million units.[[30]](#cite_note-RIAA-30) In December 2007, Ne-Yo and the [Goo Goo Dolls](/wiki/Goo_Goo_Dolls "Goo Goo Dolls") performed at a fund-raising concert for the then–presidential candidate Senator [Barack Obama](/wiki/Barack_Obama "Barack Obama").[[32]](#cite_note-32)

### 2008–2010: *Year of the Gentleman* and *Libra Scale*

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ne-Yo&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: 2008–2010: Year of the Gentleman and Libra Scale")]

Ne-Yo's third album, *[Year of the Gentleman](/wiki/Year_of_the_Gentleman "Year of the Gentleman")*, was released internationally on August 5, 2008. Speaking to noted UK R&B writer Pete Lewis of the award-winning [Blues & Soul](/wiki/Blues_%26_Soul "Blues & Soul"), Ne-Yo explained the thinking behind its title: "To me *Year of the Gentleman* is all about a persona, a swag and a charm. I made an assessment of the music business. And, in my personal opinion, the essence of the gentleman is absent right now. Everybody kinda looks the same, everybody's kinda doing the same thing, everybody's kinda rude and full of themselves. Whereas a gentleman is calm, courteous, kind, charming... So that title basically represents me trying to lead by example, and showing these cats what it is to be a gentleman in this business still."[[33]](#cite_note-33)

The album sold 250,000 copies in its first week in the United States, debuting on the *Billboard* 200 at number two.[[34]](#cite_note-34) Reviews for the album were positive: in one, Caryn Ganz of *Rolling Stone* magazine wrote that *Year of the Gentleman* is "actually a superb concept album about what a great boyfriend he [Ne-Yo] can be".[[35]](#cite_note-35) The first two singles, "[Closer](/wiki/Closer_(Ne-Yo_song) "Closer (Ne-Yo song)")" and "[Miss Independent](/wiki/Miss_Independent_(Ne-Yo_song) "Miss Independent (Ne-Yo song)")", peaked at number seven on the *Billboard* Hot 100. The album was nominated for [Best Contemporary R&B Album](/wiki/Grammy_Award_for_Best_Contemporary_R%26B_Album "Grammy Award for Best Contemporary R&B Album") and [Album of the Year](/wiki/Grammy_Award_for_Album_of_the_Year "Grammy Award for Album of the Year") at the [2009 Grammy Awards](/wiki/51st_Grammy_Awards "51st Grammy Awards"), "Closer" for [Best Male Pop Vocal Performance](/wiki/Grammy_Award_for_Best_Male_Pop_Vocal_Performance "Grammy Award for Best Male Pop Vocal Performance"), and "Miss Independent" for [Best Male R&B Vocal Performance](/wiki/Grammy_Award_for_Best_Male_R%26B_Vocal_Performance "Grammy Award for Best Male R&B Vocal Performance") and [Best R&B Song](/wiki/Grammy_Award_for_Best_R%26B_Song "Grammy Award for Best R&B Song").[[36]](#cite_note-36) *Year of the Gentleman* has been certified platinum by RIAA for a shipment of more than one million copies.[[30]](#cite_note-RIAA-30) The third single, "[Mad](/wiki/Mad_(Ne-Yo_song) "Mad (Ne-Yo song)")", peaked at number 11 on the *Billboard* Hot 100.

On August 12, 2008, the [New Kids on the Block](/wiki/New_Kids_on_the_Block "New Kids on the Block") released "[Single](/wiki/Single_(New_Kids_on_the_Block_and_Ne-Yo_song) "Single (New Kids on the Block and Ne-Yo song)")", the second single from their fifth studio album, *[The Block](/wiki/The_Block_(album) "The Block (album)")*, which is a duet with Ne-Yo. Ne-Yo included a solo version of the song on his album *Year of the Gentleman*. In December 2008, Ne-Yo performed at the Kennedy Center Honors as part of the tribute to [Barbra Streisand](/wiki/Barbra_Streisand "Barbra Streisand"); he sang and danced to the song "[Lover Come Back to Me](/wiki/Lover_Come_Back_to_Me "Lover Come Back to Me")", which Streisand recorded on her second album in 1963. In 2009, *Billboard* ranked him as the 57th Artist of the 2000s decade.[[37]](#cite_note-37) On September 2, 2009, Ne-Yo released greatest hits album *[Ne-Yo: The Collection](/wiki/Ne-Yo:_The_Collection "Ne-Yo: The Collection")* in Japan.[[38]](#cite_note-38) The album was also released with a limited edition CD+DVD edition complete with the music videos of singles.[[39]](#cite_note-39) It debuted at number four on Japan's *[Oricon](/wiki/Oricon "Oricon")* weekly albums chart, selling 55,625 copies in the first week.[[40]](#cite_note-40)

In January 2010, Ne-Yo was featured in a duet with singer [Mariah Carey](/wiki/Mariah_Carey "Mariah Carey") titled "[Angels Cry](/wiki/Angels_Cry_(song) "Angels Cry (song)")". In June 2010, Ne-Yo released the single "[Beautiful Monster](/wiki/Beautiful_Monster "Beautiful Monster")", which became Ne-Yo's third number-one single on the [UK Singles Chart](/wiki/UK_Singles_Chart "UK Singles Chart"). It peaked at number 53 on the [*Billboard* Hot 100](/wiki/Billboard_Hot_100 "Billboard Hot 100") chart. On October 8, 2010, Ne-Yo performed some of his songs from the new album at P.C. Richard & Son Theater in New York City, an event hosted by [iHeartRadio](/wiki/IHeartRadio "IHeartRadio").[[41]](#cite_note-41) Ne-Yo made an appearance at New York ComicCon to announce that he was collaborating with Stan Lee for a Libra Scale comic.[[42]](#cite_note-42) Ne-Yo also met with fans for pictures and autographs in the Cultyard of the ComicCon.[[43]](#cite_note-43) The second and third singles released from the album are "[Champagne Life](/wiki/Champagne_Life "Champagne Life")" and "[One in a Million](/wiki/One_in_a_Million_(Ne-Yo_song) "One in a Million (Ne-Yo song)")", respectively.

*[Libra Scale](/wiki/Libra_Scale "Libra Scale")* was released on November 22, 2010. It received critical acclaim from music critics, but was a commercial disappointment, debuting at number nine on the [US *Billboard* 200](/wiki/Billboard_200 "Billboard 200") chart, selling less than all of his previous three studio albums. Ne-Yo stated: "The album is based upon this short story which basically follows these three characters who are forced to choose between money-power-and-fame versus love. Which in turn is why I've called it 'Libra Scale'. You know, its whole concept is based on that question of morality – that, if you weighed it all out on a libra scale, which one of those two options would you choose?".[[44]](#cite_note-44) *Libra Scale* sold 112,000 copies in its first week. In the United Kingdom, the album debuted at number 11 on the [UK Albums Chart](/wiki/UK_Albums_Chart "UK Albums Chart"), while debuting at number one on the [UK R&B Chart](/wiki/UK_R%26B_Chart "UK R&B Chart").

### 2011–2012: *R.E.D.*

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ne-Yo&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: 2011–2012: R.E.D.")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/3/39/Ne-Yo_in_the_studio.jpg/250px-Ne-Yo_in_the_studio.jpg)](/wiki/File:Ne-Yo_in_the_studio.jpg)

Ne-Yo in a recording studio in 2011

On February 25, 2011, while on his tour in the United Kingdom, he announced his new album would be called *Love and Passion* and would be released in September, but this was proven false as the title was tentative. American rapper [Fabolous](/wiki/Fabolous "Fabolous") said in an interview that he is planning a collaboration album with Ne-Yo as well. Ne-Yo has also written songs for [Mary J. Blige](/wiki/Mary_J._Blige "Mary J. Blige"), [JB](/wiki/JB_(South_Korean_singer) "JB (South Korean singer)"), [Beyoncé](/wiki/Beyonc%C3%A9 "Beyoncé"), [Monica](/wiki/Monica_(entertainer) "Monica (entertainer)"), [Alexandra Burke](/wiki/Alexandra_Burke "Alexandra Burke"), [Cheryl Cole](/wiki/Cheryl_Cole "Cheryl Cole"), and [Willow Smith](/wiki/Willow_Smith "Willow Smith")'s upcoming albums. Ne-Yo starred in two motion pictures, the long-gestating [George Lucas](/wiki/George_Lucas "George Lucas") project *[Red Tails](/wiki/Red_Tails "Red Tails")*, released in early 2012, and *[Battle: Los Angeles](/wiki/Battle:_Los_Angeles "Battle: Los Angeles")*, which was released in the United States on March 11, 2011. Ne-Yo has already finished writing songs for American singer and [Roc Nation](/wiki/Roc_Nation "Roc Nation") artist [Alexis Jordan](/wiki/Alexis_Jordan_(singer) "Alexis Jordan (singer)") and her [self-titled debut album](/wiki/Alexis_Jordan_(album) "Alexis Jordan (album)") as well as [Jennifer Hudson](/wiki/Jennifer_Hudson "Jennifer Hudson") for her second studio album, *[I Remember Me](/wiki/I_Remember_Me_(album) "I Remember Me (album)")*, which had a North American release on March 22, 2011. Ne-Yo appeared in the children's preschool show *[The Fresh Beat Band](/wiki/The_Fresh_Beat_Band "The Fresh Beat Band")* and was one of the few artists not to cancel appearances in the wake of the [2011 Japan earthquake](/wiki/2011_Japan_earthquake "2011 Japan earthquake").[[45]](#cite_note-45)

In the spring of 2011, Ne-Yo featured alongside [Nayer](/wiki/Nayer "Nayer") and [Afrojack](/wiki/Afrojack "Afrojack") on [Pitbull](/wiki/Pitbull_(rapper) "Pitbull (rapper)")'s single "[Give Me Everything](/wiki/Give_Me_Everything "Give Me Everything")", which peaked at number one on the *Billboard* Hot 100, giving Ne-Yo his second US number-one single, his first since 2006's "[So Sick](/wiki/So_Sick "So Sick")" and his first as a guest artist. Ne-Yo also revealed in an interview that he would like to collaborate with [Chris Brown](/wiki/Chris_Brown "Chris Brown"), [Lil Wayne](/wiki/Lil_Wayne "Lil Wayne"), and [Drake](/wiki/Drake_(musician) "Drake (musician)") on his upcoming album.

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/9/95/Ne-Yo_Performs_New_Album_R.E.D._on_Walmart_Soundcheck.jpg/250px-Ne-Yo_Performs_New_Album_R.E.D._on_Walmart_Soundcheck.jpg)](/wiki/File:Ne-Yo_Performs_New_Album_R.E.D._on_Walmart_Soundcheck.jpg)

Ne-Yo performing songs from *R.E.D.* in 2012

In January 2012, it was reported that Ne-Yo, along with his label imprint Compound Entertainment, had moved from [Def Jam Recordings](/wiki/Def_Jam_Recordings "Def Jam Recordings") to [Motown Records](/wiki/Motown_Records "Motown Records").[[46]](#cite_note-46)[[47]](#cite_note-47) He was also appointed vice president of Motown, serving as a producer, songwriter, mentor, and talent scout.[[48]](#cite_note-48) Ne-Yo was slated to release his fifth studio album, *[R.E.D.](/wiki/R.E.D._(Ne-Yo_album) "R.E.D. (Ne-Yo album)")* (as in "Realizing Every Dream"), on September 18, 2012.[[49]](#cite_note-49)[[50]](#cite_note-50) The album was initially titled *The Cracks in Mr. Perfect*.[[51]](#cite_note-51) The album's first official single, "Lazy Love", was released on June 12, 2012, and peaked at number forty-two on the US [Hot R&B/Hip-Hop Songs](/wiki/Hot_R%26B/Hip-Hop_Songs "Hot R&B/Hip-Hop Songs") chart. "Let Me Love You (Until You Learn to Love Yourself)", co-written by Australian singer-songwriter [Sia](/wiki/Sia "Sia"), was released as the second single on July 31, 2012. The song peaked at number seven on the [Billboard Hot 100](/wiki/Billboard_Hot_100 "Billboard Hot 100") and became Ne-Yo's fifth number-one song in the United Kingdom.[[52]](#cite_note-52) Because of [Kevin McCall](/wiki/Kevin_McCall "Kevin McCall")'s new album and the song Ne-Yo will be in, "Love Time", was not yet released because he and his friend Chris Brown will make an appearance on Ne-Yo's R.E.D. album in the song called "What I Do", which they all will be involved in writing the beat says Ne-Yo, who was interviewed by TMZ in New York. "I expect this to be a banger in clubs for the slow songs and to get Ne-Yo his first top 10 song in years," said Chris Brown, after a celebrity basketball game with Ne-Yo, Kevin Hart, and other celebrities in the month of July.

On August 29, 2012, Ne-Yo was one of the headliners alongside [R&B](/wiki/R%26B "R&B") recording artist [Melanie Fiona](/wiki/Melanie_Fiona "Melanie Fiona"), in the *Summer Beats Concert Series*, otherwise known as the *MJ Birthday Concert*, which was on what would have been the 54th birthday of the late American pop icon [Michael Jackson](/wiki/Michael_Jackson "Michael Jackson") which was sponsored by and live-streamed by [Pepsi](/wiki/Pepsi "Pepsi") and *[Billboard](/wiki/Billboard_(magazine) "Billboard (magazine)")*. He performed a medley of hits, as well as performing "[The Way You Make Me Feel](/wiki/The_Way_You_Make_Me_Feel "The Way You Make Me Feel")", "[Smooth Criminal](/wiki/Smooth_Criminal "Smooth Criminal")" and "[I Just Can't Stop Loving You](/wiki/I_Just_Can%27t_Stop_Loving_You "I Just Can't Stop Loving You")" from Jackson's best-selling 1987 album, *[Bad](/wiki/Bad_(Michael_Jackson_album) "Bad (Michael Jackson album)")*, in preparation for the [reissue](/wiki/Reissue "Reissue") of *[Bad 25](/wiki/Bad_25 "Bad 25")* on September 18, 2012. The concert took place at [Gotham Hall](/wiki/Gotham_Hall "Gotham Hall") in New York City.[[53]](#cite_note-53) *[R.E.D.](/wiki/R.E.D._(Ne-Yo_album) "R.E.D. (Ne-Yo album)")* was released in the United Kingdom on November 5, and in North America on November 6, 2012.[[54]](#cite_note-54) On December 8, 2012, Ne-Yo was the musical guest for [Saturday Night Live](/wiki/Saturday_Night_Live "Saturday Night Live") with [Jamie Foxx](/wiki/Jamie_Foxx "Jamie Foxx") guest-hosting for that week.[[55]](#cite_note-55)

In 2012, Ne-Yo was awarded the Hal David Starlight Award of the [Songwriter's Hall of Fame](/wiki/Songwriter%27s_Hall_of_Fame "Songwriter's Hall of Fame") which honors gifted songwriters who are at an apex in their careers and are making a significant impact in the music industry via their original songs.[[56]](#cite_note-songwritershalloffame.org-56)

### 2013–2019: *Non-Fiction* and *Good Man*

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ne-Yo&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: 2013–2019: Non-Fiction and Good Man")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/6/6b/Ne-Yo_%2815329874953%29.jpg/250px-Ne-Yo_%2815329874953%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:Ne-Yo_(15329874953).jpg)

Ne-Yo performing at the National Christmas Tree lighting ceremony in December 2014

In March 2013, Ne-Yo collaborated with [Akon](/wiki/Akon "Akon") on [David Guetta](/wiki/David_Guetta "David Guetta")'s single "[Play Hard](/wiki/Play_Hard "Play Hard")", from his 2012 album *Nothing but the Beat 2.0*. He also collaborated with [Jessica Sanchez](/wiki/Jessica_Sanchez "Jessica Sanchez") on her single "[Tonight](/wiki/Tonight_(Jessica_Sanchez_song) "Tonight (Jessica Sanchez song)")" from her debut album *[Me, You & the Music](/wiki/Me,_You_%26_the_Music "Me, You & the Music")*. The single, along with the music video was released on March 21, 2013. In April, Ne-Yo and [Cher Lloyd](/wiki/Cher_Lloyd "Cher Lloyd") collaborated on their single "It's All Good", which eventually was featured on Fruttare commercials.[[57]](#cite_note-57) Around the same time, he and [Celine Dion](/wiki/Celine_Dion "Celine Dion") recorded "[Incredible](/wiki/Incredible_(Celine_Dion_and_Ne-Yo_song) "Incredible (Celine Dion and Ne-Yo song)")" for Dion's 2013 album *[Loved Me Back to Life](/wiki/Loved_Me_Back_to_Life "Loved Me Back to Life")*. "Incredible" was released as a single in February 2014 and the music video premiered in June 2014.[[58]](#cite_note-58)

On May 29, 2014, Ne-Yo announced his upcoming sixth studio album would be called *[Non-Fiction](/wiki/Non-Fiction_(Ne-Yo_album) "Non-Fiction (Ne-Yo album)")*. That same day, he announced a new single, "[Money Can't Buy](/wiki/Money_Can%27t_Buy_(song) "Money Can't Buy (song)")".[[59]](#cite_note-59) On September 16, 2014, the lead single from "Non Fiction" was sent to mainstream radio titled "[She Knows](/wiki/She_Knows_(Ne-Yo_song) "She Knows (Ne-Yo song)")" featuring [Juicy J](/wiki/Juicy_J "Juicy J").[[60]](#cite_note-60) A few months later, Ne-Yo announced that the new release date for *Non-Fiction* was January 27, 2015.[[61]](#cite_note-61) The album included collaborations with Pitbull and [Charisse Mills](/wiki/Charisse_Mills "Charisse Mills").[[62]](#cite_note-bet-62) The album peaked at number five on the *Billboard* 200 chart.

In July 2015, Ne-Yo collaborated with [Dimitri Vegas & Like Mike](/wiki/Dimitri_Vegas_%26_Like_Mike "Dimitri Vegas & Like Mike") on a new song for the [Tomorrowland](/wiki/Tomorrowland_(festival) "Tomorrowland (festival)") anthem titled "[Higher Place](/wiki/Higher_Place "Higher Place")".[[63]](#cite_note-63) In 2016, Ne-Yo collaborated with Yunel and [J Alvarez](/wiki/J_Alvarez "J Alvarez") for their single entitled "Sin Miedo", released on November 12, 2016, and with [Diamond Platnumz](/wiki/Diamond_Platnumz "Diamond Platnumz") for their single entitled "Marry You", released on February 2, 2017.

On May 8, 2017, Ne-Yo released a single "[Earn Your Love](/wiki/Earn_Your_Love "Earn Your Love")" from his forthcoming seventh album, whilst announcing that the album is slated for a release in 2017.[[64]](#cite_note-64)[[65]](#cite_note-65) A new single, "[Another Love Song](/wiki/Another_Love_Song_(Ne-Yo_song) "Another Love Song (Ne-Yo song)")" was released on May 30, 2017.[[66]](#cite_note-66) He also made a cameo in the comedy film *[Girls Trip](/wiki/Girls_Trip "Girls Trip")*, starring [Queen Latifah](/wiki/Queen_Latifah "Queen Latifah"), [Jada Pinkett Smith](/wiki/Jada_Pinkett_Smith "Jada Pinkett Smith"), and [Regina Hall](/wiki/Regina_Hall "Regina Hall").[[67]](#cite_note-67)

### 2020–present: New music, *The Masked Singer*, and Broadway debut

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ne-Yo&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: 2020–present: New music, The Masked Singer, and Broadway debut")]

On March 27, 2020, Ne-Yo collaborated with Jamaican [dancehall](/wiki/Dancehall "Dancehall") singer [Charly Black](/wiki/Charly_Black "Charly Black") for a new single, *Over Again*.[[68]](#cite_note-68)

From 2020 to 2021, Ne-Yo competed as "Badger" in the [second season](/wiki/The_Masked_Singer_(British_series_2) "The Masked Singer (British series 2)") of the [British version](/wiki/The_Masked_Singer_(British_TV_series) "The Masked Singer (British TV series)") of *[The Masked Singer](/wiki/The_Masked_Singer "The Masked Singer")*. He finished as the runner-up to [Joss Stone](/wiki/Joss_Stone "Joss Stone") as "Sausage". He also confirmed that his next album, previously titled *Sweet Escape* had been renamed *[Self Explanatory](/wiki/Self_Explanatory_(Ne-Yo_album) "Self Explanatory (Ne-Yo album)")*.[[69]](#cite_note-69) It was released on [Motown Records](/wiki/Motown_Records "Motown Records") on July 15, 2022.[[70]](#cite_note-70)

In 2023, Ne-Yo competed in [season ten](/wiki/The_Masked_Singer_(American_season_10) "The Masked Singer (American season 10)") of *[The Masked Singer](/wiki/The_Masked_Singer_(American_TV_series) "The Masked Singer (American TV series)")* as "Cow" where he won the season.[[71]](#cite_note-71)

On December 4, 2025, Ne-Yo made his Broadway debut in [Hell's Kitchen](/wiki/Hell%27s_Kitchen_(musical) "Hell's Kitchen (musical)"), a semi-autobiographical jukebox musical by [Alicia Keys](/wiki/Alicia_Keys "Alicia Keys"), in the role of Davis.[[72]](#cite_note-72)

## Artistry

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ne-Yo&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Artistry")]

### Musical style and influences

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ne-Yo&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Musical style and influences")]

Ne-Yo's music is generally [R&B](/wiki/Contemporary_R%26B "Contemporary R&B"), while he also incorporates pop and [hip hop](/wiki/Hip_hop_music "Hip hop music") occasionally. His musical influences include [Michael Jackson](/wiki/Michael_Jackson "Michael Jackson"), [Luther Vandross](/wiki/Luther_Vandross "Luther Vandross"), [Babyface](/wiki/Babyface_(musician) "Babyface (musician)"), [Blackstreet](/wiki/Blackstreet "Blackstreet"), and [Usher](/wiki/Usher_(musician) "Usher (musician)").[[73]](#cite_note-73)[[74]](#cite_note-74) Ne-Yo is noted for his [tenor](/wiki/Tenor "Tenor") voice, including his application of [falsetto](/wiki/Falsetto "Falsetto") in his works.[[75]](#cite_note-75)[[76]](#cite_note-76)[[77]](#cite_note-77)[[78]](#cite_note-78)

### Songwriting and production

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ne-Yo&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Songwriting and production")]

Ne-Yo frequently co-writes with [Tor Erik Hermansen](/wiki/Tor_Erik_Hermansen "Tor Erik Hermansen") and Mikkel S. Eriksen of the Norwegian production team [Stargate](/wiki/Stargate_(production_team) "Stargate (production team)"). Ne-Yo met them in a hallway at [Sony Music Studios](/wiki/Sony_Music_Studios "Sony Music Studios") in New York, and knowing the team produced R&B records, decided to collaborate with them. The collective's early works were tracks from *In My Own Words*, including "So Sick".[[79]](#cite_note-79)

Aside from working on his own album, Ne-Yo also collaborates with several other artists. His works include: [Rihanna](/wiki/Rihanna "Rihanna")'s top-ten singles "[Unfaithful](/wiki/Unfaithful_(song) "Unfaithful (song)")", "[Russian Roulette](/wiki/Russian_Roulette_(Rihanna_song) "Russian Roulette (Rihanna song)")", and her number-one hit "[Take a Bow](/wiki/Take_a_Bow_(Rihanna_song) "Take a Bow (Rihanna song)")", [Mario Vazquez](/wiki/Mario_Vazquez "Mario Vazquez")'s "[Gallery](/wiki/Gallery_(Mario_Vazquez_song) "Gallery (Mario Vazquez song)")", [Paula DeAnda](/wiki/Paula_DeAnda "Paula DeAnda")'s "[Walk Away (Remember Me)](/wiki/Walk_Away_(Remember_Me) "Walk Away (Remember Me)")", and [Beyoncé Knowles](/wiki/Beyonc%C3%A9_Knowles "Beyoncé Knowles")' *Billboard* Hot 100 number-one single "[Irreplaceable](/wiki/Irreplaceable "Irreplaceable")", which stayed at the top of the chart for ten consecutive weeks.[[80]](#cite_note-HobbsSex-80) He wrote the song "I'm You" for [Leona Lewis](/wiki/Leona_Lewis "Leona Lewis")'s debut album *[Spirit](/wiki/Spirit_(Leona_Lewis_album) "Spirit (Leona Lewis album)")*, and is currently writing songs for her next album and for the debut album of fellow [X Factor](/wiki/The_X_Factor_(UK) "The X Factor (UK)") winner [Alexandra Burke](/wiki/Alexandra_Burke "Alexandra Burke"). He has also been working with [Sugababes](/wiki/Sugababes "Sugababes") for their [seventh album](/wiki/Sweet_7 "Sweet 7"), with member [Keisha Buchanan](/wiki/Keisha_Buchanan "Keisha Buchanan") confirming that Ne-Yo had written a song called "No More You" for their album.[[81]](#cite_note-81)

In 2010, he dueted with [Mariah Carey](/wiki/Mariah_Carey "Mariah Carey") on "Angel's Cry", a song for her cancelled album, *Angels Advocate*. Ne-Yo has also written songs for [Celine Dion](/wiki/Celine_Dion "Celine Dion"), [Whitney Houston](/wiki/Whitney_Houston "Whitney Houston"), [Carrie Underwood](/wiki/Carrie_Underwood "Carrie Underwood"), [Anastacia](/wiki/Anastacia "Anastacia"), [Ciara](/wiki/Ciara "Ciara"), [Corbin Bleu](/wiki/Corbin_Bleu "Corbin Bleu"), [Enrique Iglesias](/wiki/Enrique_Iglesias "Enrique Iglesias"), and [Dima Bilan](/wiki/Dima_Bilan "Dima Bilan").[[80]](#cite_note-HobbsSex-80) He collaborated with [Lindsay Lohan](/wiki/Lindsay_Lohan "Lindsay Lohan") on her new material, having already finished "[Bossy](/wiki/Bossy_(Lindsay_Lohan_song) "Bossy (Lindsay Lohan song)")", a pre-single for her new album *Spirit in the Dark*.[[82]](#cite_note-82) In 2007, Ne-Yo confirmed that he had been contacted by producer [will.i.am](/wiki/Will.i.am "Will.i.am") to work on what would have been [Michael Jackson](/wiki/Michael_Jackson "Michael Jackson")'s new album.[[83]](#cite_note-83) However, at the time of Jackson's death, Ne-Yo's collaborations with him had yet to move past the writing stage.[[84]](#cite_note-84) In an interview in 2010, Ne-Yo said that, since Jackson's death, he had been confused as what to do with the songs, as he felt that selling them to another artist or even releasing the songs himself would be disrespectful to Jackson's legacy.[[85]](#cite_note-85) In 2009, he wrote "[Truth (Saigo no Shinjitsu)](/wiki/New_World/Truth_(Saigo_no_Shinjitsu) "New World/Truth (Saigo no Shinjitsu)")" for the Japanese pop group [w-inds.](/wiki/W-inds. "W-inds.")[[86]](#cite_note-86)

Ne-Yo ventured out to open his own recording studio, called Carrington House, in Atlanta, Georgia.[[87]](#cite_note-FrezzaOpen-87) He also had started his own production company, Compound Entertainment, in 2007, and has hired several producers and songwriters in hopes of turning it into a full-fledged [record label](/wiki/Record_label "Record label"). It successfully became a label, and artists such as [RaVaughn](/wiki/RaVaughn "RaVaughn"), [Paula Campbell](/wiki/Paula_Campbell_(singer) "Paula Campbell (singer)"), Sixx John, [Adrienne Bailon](/wiki/Adrienne_Bailon "Adrienne Bailon") and Shanell are now associated with Compound.[[23]](#cite_note-JonesTunes-23)[[87]](#cite_note-FrezzaOpen-87)

## Other ventures

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ne-Yo&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Other ventures")]

### Television

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ne-Yo&action=edit&section=13 "Edit section: Television")]

In 2011, Ne-Yo was approached by [Cartoon Network](/wiki/Cartoon_Network "Cartoon Network") to help create an animated series. Ne-Yo announced that the show would be titled *I Heart Tuesday* and that he had created it for his sister.[[88]](#cite_note-mtv-88) He also stated that he wanted to avoid having the show resemble the [anime](/wiki/Anime "Anime") art style of *[The Boondocks](/wiki/The_Boondocks_(TV_series) "The Boondocks (TV series)")* due to the potential cost of the show's animation style.[[88]](#cite_note-mtv-88)[[89]](#cite_note-89) The series has since been believed to have been quietly cancelled.

Ne-Yo appeared in an episode of [the seventh season](/wiki/CSI:_NY_(season_7) "CSI: NY (season 7)") of *[CSI: NY](/wiki/CSI:_NY "CSI: NY")* as a hit-man, under his birth name of Shaffer Smith.[[90]](#cite_note-90)[[91]](#cite_note-91)

In 2011, Ne-Yo appeared in an episode of *[The Fresh Beat Band](/wiki/The_Fresh_Beat_Band "The Fresh Beat Band")* as himself.[[92]](#cite_note-92)

He starred as the [Tin Man](/wiki/Tin_Woodman "Tin Woodman") in [NBC](/wiki/NBC "NBC")'s [musical telecast adaptation](/wiki/The_Wiz_Live! "The Wiz Live!") of *[The Wiz](/wiki/The_Wiz "The Wiz")* alongside [David Alan Grier](/wiki/David_Alan_Grier "David Alan Grier"), [Elijah Kelley](/wiki/Elijah_Kelley "Elijah Kelley"), [Queen Latifah](/wiki/Queen_Latifah "Queen Latifah"), [Common](/wiki/Common_(rapper) "Common (rapper)"), [Amber Riley](/wiki/Amber_Riley "Amber Riley"), [Uzo Aduba](/wiki/Uzo_Aduba "Uzo Aduba") and [Mary J. Blige](/wiki/Mary_J._Blige "Mary J. Blige"). The musical telecast premiered December 3, 2015 as a three-hour event. For his performance, he received a [Critics' Choice Award](/wiki/Critics%27_Choice_Movie_Awards "Critics' Choice Movie Awards") nomination for Best Supporting Actor in a Movie made for Television or Limited Series.[[93]](#cite_note-93) In May 2018, he took part in the [Celebrity Ninja Warrior](/wiki/American_Ninja_Warrior "American Ninja Warrior") special for [Red Nose Day](/wiki/Red_Nose_Day "Red Nose Day"). He completed the course, resulting in a $30,000 donation.[[94]](#cite_note-94)

In December 2018, he was the entertainment at [67th Miss Universe pageant](/wiki/Miss_Universe_2018 "Miss Universe 2018") where he performed during the opening and final catwalk.

### Philanthropy

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ne-Yo&action=edit&section=14 "Edit section: Philanthropy")]

Ne-Yo is passionate about the arts and children and has shown his support for [Little Kids Rock](/wiki/Little_Kids_Rock "Little Kids Rock") by filming a PSA for the organization.[[95]](#cite_note-95)

### Sports management

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ne-Yo&action=edit&section=15 "Edit section: Sports management")]

In November 2024, Ne-Yo began a stint in sports management, signing boxer [Paul Bamba](/wiki/Paul_Bamba "Paul Bamba") as his first fighter. Ne-Yo was initially introduced to boxing through his uncles when he was younger and he participated in martial arts classes. Later in life, boxing was incorporated into his workout routine, where he would meet Bamba.[[96]](#cite_note-96) Bamba would become the [World Boxing Association](/wiki/World_Boxing_Association "World Boxing Association")'s cruiserweight champion in December 2024, and broke [Mike Tyson's](/wiki/Mike_Tyson "Mike Tyson") record for most boxing wins in a calendar year via knockout, with 14 KO's. Bamba died on December 27th, 2024, 6 days after winning the championship.[[97]](#cite_note-97)

## Personal life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ne-Yo&action=edit&section=16 "Edit section: Personal life")]

### Family

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ne-Yo&action=edit&section=17 "Edit section: Family")]

In 2005, Jessica White, Ne-Yo's girlfriend at the time, gave birth to a boy.[[80]](#cite_note-HobbsSex-80) Ne-Yo later discovered the child was not his.[[98]](#cite_note-98)[[99]](#cite_note-99)

In June 2010, Ne-Yo told *[Ebony](/wiki/Ebony_(magazine) "Ebony (magazine)")* that he and his then-girlfriend, Monyetta Shaw, were expecting their first child together – a girl due early in 2011.[[100]](#cite_note-100) Shaw gave birth prematurely to a daughter in November 2010. Announcing the birth, Ne-Yo said, "I've been in love before but this feels like nothing I've ever felt... like I'm in love for the first time."[[101]](#cite_note-101) Shaw and Ne-Yo's son was born in October 2011.[[102]](#cite_note-102) They broke up in 2013.[[103]](#cite_note-s826-103)

In September 2015, the singer announced that he and Crystal Renay Williams were engaged and expecting a child.[[104]](#cite_note-104) They were married on February 20, 2016.[[105]](#cite_note-105) A son was born to them in March 2016.[[106]](#cite_note-106) Their second son was born in June 2018.[[107]](#cite_note-107) In February 2020, Ne-Yo announced that he and Crystal would end their marriage,[[108]](#cite_note-108) and he withdrew his divorce case afterwards.[[109]](#cite_note-109) The pair reconciled during the onset of the [COVID-19 pandemic](/wiki/COVID-19_pandemic "COVID-19 pandemic") in 2020, with Ne-Yo telling CBS chat show *The Talk*: "Before the quarantine happened, we were definitely talking divorce, and the quarantine forced us to sit still, block out the noise from the world – you know the world can get very, very loud, and we tend to let the world's opinion mean more in certain situations than it should."[[110]](#cite_note-110)[[111]](#cite_note-111)

Ne-Yo and Crystal's third child, a daughter, was born in June 2021.[[112]](#cite_note-112) In April 2022, the couple renewed their vows in a Las Vegas ceremony.[[113]](#cite_note-113) Four months later, Crystal filed for divorce, with allegations of Ne-Yo fathering a child outside of marriage.[[114]](#cite_note-114) On January 26, 2023, Ne-Yo and Crystal finalized their divorce.[[115]](#cite_note-115) Ne-Yo fathered two sons, born in 2021 and 2023, to influencer Sade Jenea Bagnerise.[[2]](#cite_note-children-2)

In 2019, in wake of the [television documentary](/wiki/Television_documentary "Television documentary"), *[Surviving R. Kelly](/wiki/Surviving_R._Kelly "Surviving R. Kelly")*, Ne-Yo confirmed his support for the [Mute R. Kelly](/wiki/Mute_R._Kelly "Mute R. Kelly") campaign.[[116]](#cite_note-116)

In 2025, he revealed being in a [polyamorous](/wiki/Polyamorous "Polyamorous") relationship with four women.[[117]](#cite_note-117)[[118]](#cite_note-118)

### Legal issues

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ne-Yo&action=edit&section=18 "Edit section: Legal issues")]

On February 19, 2008, Ne-Yo was arrested for [reckless driving](/wiki/Reckless_driving "Reckless driving") and driving without a valid license after driving through [Cobb County, Georgia](/wiki/Cobb_County,_Georgia "Cobb County, Georgia") in his 2006 [Range Rover](/wiki/Range_Rover "Range Rover"). It was reported that he was doing about 150 mph, 100 mph over the speed limit. On June 2, 2008, Ne-Yo pleaded guilty to the driving without a valid license charge and no contest to the reckless driving charge. He was sentenced to 24 hours of community service.[[119]](#cite_note-119)

In 1998, Ne-Yo signed a management agreement with David Wallace for seven years that Wallace would provide professional management services for Ne-Yo in exchange for 15%. But shortly after, the singer moved to [Los Angeles](/wiki/Los_Angeles "Los Angeles") where his career started to blossom.[[120]](#cite_note-120) Tanya Wallace maintains David Wallace tried to contact Ne-Yo several times by telephone but was unsuccessful. In 2007, Wallace filed a complaint against Smith in district court, seeking damages for breach of contract and other claims. While litigation was pending, Wallace divorced his wife, Tanya, and the divorce decree awarded Tanya one-half of the proceeds from any future judgment in the lawsuit. The district court later dismissed the case and while Wallace's appeal was pending, he died in 2012.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*][[121]](#cite_note-121)

On August 22, 2011, Ne-Yo was sued along with rapper [Pitbull](/wiki/Pitbull_(rapper) "Pitbull (rapper)") and DJ [Afrojack](/wiki/Afrojack "Afrojack") by actress [Lindsay Lohan](/wiki/Lindsay_Lohan "Lindsay Lohan"), claiming that their song "Give Me Everything" referenced her by name in the lyrics. A judge ruled that Pitbull's use of her name was protected by the [First Amendment](/wiki/First_Amendment "First Amendment") and that Lohan was barely mentioned in the song. Pitbull then won the lawsuit.[[122]](#cite_note-122)

On March 22, 2023, Ne-Yo was revealed to have been charged by the [United States Securities and Exchange Commission](/wiki/United_States_Securities_and_Exchange_Commission "United States Securities and Exchange Commission") for violating investor-protection laws. Ne-Yo settled the charges for over $400,000 without admitting or denying the claims.[[123]](#cite_note-123)[[124]](#cite_note-124)

### Health

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ne-Yo&action=edit&section=19 "Edit section: Health")]

In August 2017, Ne-Yo announced he had adopted a [vegan](/wiki/Veganism "Veganism") diet after viewing the documentary *[What the Health](/wiki/What_the_Health "What the Health")*. In February 2019, he said in an interview that he had lost "like 30 pounds" and had also reversed his [tendinitis](/wiki/Tendinopathy "Tendinopathy") since he became vegan, calling it "one of the best things he's ever done".[[125]](#cite_note-125) However, his representative confirmed in March 2020 that he was no longer a vegan.[[126]](#cite_note-126)

### Comments about transgender youth

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ne-Yo&action=edit&section=20 "Edit section: Comments about transgender youth")]

During a television appearance in 2023, Ne-Yo made comments that were critical of [transgender youth](/wiki/Transgender_youth "Transgender youth") and [gender affirming care](/wiki/Gender_affirming_care "Gender affirming care") for children.[[127]](#cite_note-LATimes-127)[[128]](#cite_note-USA-128) He later posted an apology for his comments.[[127]](#cite_note-LATimes-127)[[128]](#cite_note-USA-128) The following day, Ne-Yo retracted his apology and suggested that the apology had come from a publicist.[[128]](#cite_note-USA-128)[[129]](#cite_note-Rolling-129)

## Awards and nominations

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ne-Yo&action=edit&section=26 "Edit section: Awards and nominations")]

Main article: [List of awards and nominations received by Ne-Yo](/wiki/List_of_awards_and_nominations_received_by_Ne-Yo "List of awards and nominations received by Ne-Yo")

Ne-Yo has received several nominations at the [Grammy Awards](/wiki/Grammy_Award "Grammy Award") during his career, winning three; [Best Contemporary R&B Album](/wiki/Grammy_Award_for_Best_Contemporary_R%26B_Album "Grammy Award for Best Contemporary R&B Album") for *Because of You*, [Best Male R&B Vocal Performance](/wiki/Grammy_Award_for_Best_Male_R%26B_Vocal_Performance "Grammy Award for Best Male R&B Vocal Performance") and [Best R&B Song](/wiki/Grammy_Award_for_Best_R%26B_Song "Grammy Award for Best R&B Song") for "[Miss Independent](/wiki/Miss_Independent_(Ne-Yo_song) "Miss Independent (Ne-Yo song)")". The [MTV Video Music Awards](/wiki/MTV_Video_Music_Awards "MTV Video Music Awards") has nominated Ne-Yo in 2008 for [Best Dancing in a Video](/wiki/Best_Dancing_in_a_Video "Best Dancing in a Video") for "Closer".
